sub_criteria.name: Common Characteristics of Good Applications
sub_criteria.definition: The application demonstrates characteristics commonly found in strong grant applications.

signals.signal_text: Clear research vision and career trajectory
Distinctive ideas with an independent direction
Well-justified training plan
Strong mentorship and institutional support

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates a clear research vision regarding trauma-informed care in EDs (g.1.a) and proposes distinctive ideas using arts-based co-design (g.1.b). There is evidence of institutional support through the contracting NHS Trust and university partnership (g.1.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application lacks a personal career trajectory or vision for the lead applicant (g.1.a). There is no explicit training plan for the applicant (g.1.c). Mentorship arrangements are not explicitly described in the provided text (g.1.d). The 'distinctive ideas' are project-focused rather than demonstrating an independent research direction for the applicant.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application lacks a personal career trajectory or vision for the lead applicant (g.1.a). There is no explicit training plan for the applicant (g.1.c). Mentorship arrangements are not explicitly described in the provided text (g.1.d). The 'distinctive ideas' are project-focused rather than demonstrating an independent research direction for the applicant.

sub_criteria.name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award
sub_criteria.definition: The applicant clearly articulates why this specific award is the right next step in their career.
sub_criteria.group_name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award

signals.signal_text: Explain why this award is the right next step in your career (not just another project grant)
What difference would securing an award make to your career, and why now?
How will an award build on your existing experience and what new skills will it enable you to gain?
How will it develop your research partnerships within the NHS, public health or social care, charity organisations, industry or other organisations?

sub_criteria.pros: The application mentions partnerships with NHS trusts and community organizations, which hints at developing research partnerships (g.2.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no evidence explaining why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant's career (g.2.a). The text does not articulate the difference securing this award would make to the applicant's career or why now (g.2.b). There is no mention of new skills the applicant will gain or how the award builds on their experience (g.2.c). The partnership evidence is project-based, not explicitly linked to the applicant's career development.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no evidence explaining why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant's career (g.2.a). The text does not articulate the difference securing this award would make to the applicant's career or why now (g.2.b). There is no mention of new skills the applicant will gain or how the award builds on their experience (g.2.c). The partnership evidence is project-based, not explicitly linked to the applicant's career development.

sub_criteria.name: Relevant Research Experience and Training
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates prior research experience and/or training that prepares the applicant to successfully undertake the proposed award in applied health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of previous projects, research methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study
Evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications
Alignment between prior experience and skills required for the proposed project

sub_criteria.pros: The lead applicant's role description mentions managing large NIHR studies, implying previous project experience (g.3.a). The team composition suggests alignment of skills for the proposed co-design work (g.3.c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no specific mention of previous projects, methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study in the applicant's personal history (g.3.a). No evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications is provided (g.3.b). The alignment of prior experience is inferred from the team structure rather than explicit applicant history (g.3.c).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no specific mention of previous projects, methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study in the applicant's personal history (g.3.a). No evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications is provided (g.3.b). The alignment of prior experience is inferred from the team structure rather than explicit applicant history (g.3.c).

sub_criteria.name: Impactful Research Outputs and Outcomes
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful research outputs or outcomes appropriate to the applicant’s career stage, demonstrating contribution to the field.

signals.signal_text: Number and quality of publications, reports, or presentations
Citation metrics or other indicators of impact (where mentioned)
Awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research
Description of practical or societal impact of previous work

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text contains no list of publications, reports, or presentations (g.4.a). There are no citation metrics or impact indicators mentioned (g.4.b). No awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research are listed (g.4.c). There is no description of the practical or societal impact of the applicant's previous work (g.4.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text contains no list of publications, reports, or presentations (g.4.a). There are no citation metrics or impact indicators mentioned (g.4.b). No awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research are listed (g.4.c). There is no description of the practical or societal impact of the applicant's previous work (g.4.d).

sub_criteria.name: Trajectory Towards Research Leadership
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates potential and progression towards becoming a future leader in health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Statements of career ambitions and long-term goals
Evidence of increasing responsibility in previous roles (e.g., leading projects, mentoring)
Participation in leadership or professional development programs
Recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential

sub_criteria.pros: The lead applicant's proposed role includes strategic leadership and project governance, suggesting some level of increasing responsibility (g.5.b).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There are no explicit statements of career ambitions or long-term goals (g.5.a). Evidence of increasing responsibility is limited to the current project role description (g.5.b). There is no mention of participation in leadership or professional development programs (g.5.c). No recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential is provided (g.5.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: There are no explicit statements of career ambitions or long-term goals (g.5.a). Evidence of increasing responsibility is limited to the current project role description (g.5.b). There is no mention of participation in leadership or professional development programs (g.5.c). No recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential is provided (g.5.d).

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Practitioner-Academic Career (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: For applicants including a clinical or practice element, shows a clear commitment to combining research with professional practice.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions
Involvement in clinical or practice-based research
Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities
Participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications

sub_criteria.pros: The inclusion of a co-applicant from the NHS Emergency Department and a PPI partner suggests involvement in practice-based research (g.6.b).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions by the lead applicant (g.6.a). The involvement in clinical research is inferred from team composition, not the applicant's own practice (g.6.b). There is no evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities (g.6.c). No participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications is mentioned (g.6.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions by the lead applicant (g.6.a). The involvement in clinical research is inferred from team composition, not the applicant's own practice (g.6.b). There is no evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities (g.6.c). No participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications is mentioned (g.6.d).

sub_criteria.name: Quality of the Plain English Summary
sub_criteria.definition: Clear, simple description of the research that a non-specialist audience can understand; explains problem, importance, plan, beneficiaries, and real-world use; avoids jargon; aligns with the detailed proposal.

signals.signal_text: Readability scores (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid)
Low density of unexplained jargon or technical terms
Presence of problem statement, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and real-world impact
Alignment with detailed proposal content
Sentence structure and coherence for non-specialist understanding
Emphasis on societal or patient relevance

sub_criteria.pros: The Plain English Summary is exceptionally clear, with NLP metrics confirming high readability (Flesch-Kincaid 7.45) and low jargon density (3.33%). It comprehensively covers the problem, objectives, methods (Experience-Based Co-Design), beneficiaries, and impact. The text explicitly states it was rewritten with survivor-experts to eliminate complex language, ensuring alignment with the detailed proposal and strong societal relevance.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats or missing details found; the summary perfectly meets all criteria for a non-specialist audience.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats or missing details found; the summary perfectly meets all criteria for a non-specialist audience.

sub_criteria.name: Evidence Review Quality & Relevance
sub_criteria.definition: How comprehensively and critically the application reviews existing literature and evidence.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of references cited
Presence of critical analysis vs. mere summary
Identification of knowledge gaps
Coherence and logical flow

sub_criteria.pros: The Scientific Abstract clearly identifies a specific knowledge gap regarding the lack of evidence for introducing trauma-informed practices into Emergency Departments. It logically connects the problem (survivors avoiding care) to the proposed solution.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The provided text (Scientific Abstract) does not contain a comprehensive literature review with specific citations or critical analysis of existing studies. The evidence review is limited to a high-level statement of the gap rather than a detailed critique of the field.
sub_criteria.rationale: The provided text (Scientific Abstract) does not contain a comprehensive literature review with specific citations or critical analysis of existing studies. The evidence review is limited to a high-level statement of the gap rather than a detailed critique of the field.

sub_criteria.name: Research Scope & Award Appropriateness
sub_criteria.definition: Whether the project scope matches the scale and expectations of the proposed award (doctoral, postdoctoral, etc.).

signals.signal_text: Alignment of aims with award level
Feasibility indicators (time, complexity, tasks)
Proportion of work relative to award expectations

sub_criteria.pros: The project scope is well-defined with a 24-month timeline, specific phases (M1-16, M17-18, M19-24), and clear sample sizes (n=20 staff, n=30 survivors). Feasibility is strongly supported by detailed mitigation strategies for staff turnover and specific time allocations (108 hours) for staff engagement.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the scope is appropriately matched to the award duration and resources.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the scope is appropriately matched to the award duration and resources.

sub_criteria.name: Research Design & Methodological Rigor
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity, appropriateness, and robustness of study design to answer the research questions.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of methods
Logical alignment between aims and methods
Discussion of controls, sample size, or methodology justification
Structured methodological sections

sub_criteria.pros: Methods are explicitly described (focus groups, narrative interviews, co-design events) and logically aligned with objectives. The application details a multi-level intervention design and includes robust safeguards for participant safety and trauma-informed recruitment, demonstrating high methodological rigor.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the qualitative methods are strong, the text does not explicitly discuss statistical controls or power calculations, though this is less relevant for a co-design feasibility study. The justification for sample sizes is qualitative rather than statistical.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the qualitative methods are strong, the text does not explicitly discuss statistical controls or power calculations, though this is less relevant for a co-design feasibility study. The justification for sample sizes is qualitative rather than statistical.

sub_criteria.name: Potential Impact on Patients, Public, and Health Services
sub_criteria.definition: Likely benefits or influence of the research on patients, carers, or health and care systems.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of direct or indirect patient/public benefits
Alignment with health priorities or societal needs
Clarity and specificity of expected outcomes

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly articulates direct benefits for survivors (improved safety, reduced distress) and the NHS (efficiencies, lower costs). It aligns with health priorities by addressing sexual violence and health inequalities, with specific expected outcomes like a theory of change and intervention prototypes.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the impact pathway is clear and specific.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the impact pathway is clear and specific.

sub_criteria.name: Engagement with People & Communities
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful involvement of patients, carers, or communities.

signals.signal_text: Use of co-design, participatory methods, or consultations
References to ethical considerations and feedback mechanisms
Description of involvement at multiple project stages

sub_criteria.pros: Engagement is deeply embedded across all stages (design, recruitment, analysis, dissemination) using Experience-Based Co-Design. A survivor-led organization (Survivors Voices) is a co-applicant, ensuring meaningful involvement. Ethical considerations and safety measures are explicitly detailed.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the engagement plan is comprehensive and well-resourced.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the engagement plan is comprehensive and well-resourced.

sub_criteria.name: Justification of Resources & Award Costs
sub_criteria.definition: Appropriateness and clarity of requested resources for the planned work.

signals.signal_text: Explicit links between tasks and resources requested
Budget proportionality to project scope
Clear rationale for funding level

sub_criteria.pros: The budget provides explicit links between tasks and resources, including detailed costs for staff time, travel, inclusion activities (interpreters, translation), and creative outputs. The rationale for funding levels is clear, with specific justifications for increased PPI time and inclusion costs.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the resource justification is thorough and proportional.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the resource justification is thorough and proportional.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of Previous Research (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: Prior relevant research experience demonstrating capability to deliver the proposed project.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of publications or outputs
Evidence of prior successful projects
Skills alignment with proposed work

sub_criteria.pros: The Application Context mentions the lead applicant has managed large NIHR studies and co-applicants have established collaborations (e.g., 5 years working together). This suggests some prior successful project experience.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The provided text does not list specific publications, prior outputs, or detailed descriptions of previous successful projects relevant to the proposed work. Evidence of prior research quality is limited to general role descriptions.
sub_criteria.rationale: The provided text does not list specific publications, prior outputs, or detailed descriptions of previous successful projects relevant to the proposed work. Evidence of prior research quality is limited to general role descriptions.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusive Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: Consideration of inclusivity throughout the research lifecycle, including participant diversity and accessibility.

signals.signal_text: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment
Adaptive methods or accessibility considerations
Equity and participation indicators

sub_criteria.pros: The application includes explicit strategies for diverse recruitment (inclusion lead, interpreters, translation into 5 languages) and adaptive methods (remote interviews, travel for carers). It specifically targets underserved groups (migrant women, cultural minorities) and budgets for these inclusion activities.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the inclusive research design is robust and well-resourced.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the inclusive research design is robust and well-resourced.

sub_criteria.name: Quality and Relevance of Proposed Training
sub_criteria.definition: How well-structured, clear, and relevant the training plan is in relation to the project’s goals and the applicant’s research needs.

signals.signal_text: Completeness and clarity of training description
Alignment of training topics with project methods or techniques
Structured timeline or sequencing of training activities
Explicit links between training content and skill acquisition

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding a specific training plan.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text and belief state contain no description of training activities, timelines, or structured learning plans. The text focuses on project roles and deliverables rather than the applicant's training development.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text and belief state contain no description of training activities, timelines, or structured learning plans. The text focuses on project roles and deliverables rather than the applicant's training development.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability to Personal Development and Project Needs
sub_criteria.definition: How well the proposed training addresses the applicant’s current skills gaps and supports successful project delivery.

signals.signal_text: Identification of personal skill gaps
Clear mapping between training activities and project tasks
Evidence that proposed training is targeted rather than generic
Mention of mentorship, workshops, or formal courses relevant to development needs

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding personal skill gaps or targeted training.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no identification of personal skill gaps, nor any mapping of training activities to project tasks. The text lists roles and responsibilities but does not articulate a development plan to address specific needs.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no identification of personal skill gaps, nor any mapping of training activities to project tasks. The text lists roles and responsibilities but does not articulate a development plan to address specific needs.

sub_criteria.name: Support for Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: The extent to which the training plan, and any professional practice components, contribute to the applicant’s long-term career goals.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of career goals
Connections between training activities and future career trajectory
Indications of skill transferability beyond the current project
Reference to professional practice or experiential learning where applicable

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding career aspirations linked to training.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text does not contain explicit statements of career goals, connections between training and future trajectory, or references to professional practice components as part of a development plan.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text does not contain explicit statements of career goals, connections between training and future trajectory, or references to professional practice components as part of a development plan.

sub_criteria.name: Track Record of the Contracting Organisation (and Partners)
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrated expertise, experience, and research achievements of the organisation(s) in the proposed research area.

signals.signal_text: Publication or project history in relevant research fields
Previous funding success or awards in similar domains
Mention of prior collaborations or partnerships
Highlighted achievements or notable outputs

sub_criteria.pros: The application lists the contracting organisation (University Hospitals Coventry and Warwickshire NHS Trust) and partner organisations (Coventry University, Reshapers CIC), but provides no specific details regarding their track record, publication history, or prior research achievements in the relevant field.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No evidence found in the provided text regarding the organisations' publication history, previous funding success, specific prior collaborations, or notable outputs. The text only lists names and departments without substantiating their expertise or achievements.
sub_criteria.rationale: No evidence found in the provided text regarding the organisations' publication history, previous funding success, specific prior collaborations, or notable outputs. The text only lists names and departments without substantiating their expertise or achievements.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability of Supervisory Support and Mentorship Arrangements
sub_criteria.definition: Quality, clarity, and appropriateness of supervision and mentoring for supporting the applicant’s research and career development.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of supervisor roles and responsibilities
Evidence of mentorship plans, meetings, or training support
Alignment between supervisor expertise and project needs
Presence of structured supervision framework (frequency, format, accountability)

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides explicit descriptions of roles for the Lead Applicant and Co-Applicants (e.g., strategic leadership, qualitative data collection, PPI input), indicating a clear division of responsibilities. The team composition includes academic, clinical, and survivor-led expertise, suggesting alignment with project needs.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no evidence of a structured supervision framework, such as meeting frequency, format, or specific mentorship plans. The text describes roles but lacks details on how supervision and career development support will be delivered or monitored.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no evidence of a structured supervision framework, such as meeting frequency, format, or specific mentorship plans. The text describes roles but lacks details on how supervision and career development support will be delivered or monitored.

sub_criteria.name: Appropriateness of the Contracting Organisation to Support the Award and Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: How well the organisation (and any partners) can provide the infrastructure, resources, and environment necessary to deliver the project and support the applicant’s career growth.

signals.signal_text: Description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided
Links between organisational support and project success
Alignment of organisational focus with applicant’s career trajectory
Evidence of tailored support for award type (doctoral, postdoctoral)

sub_criteria.pros: The application identifies the contracting organisation and partners, implying a multi-sectoral support structure.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No specific description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the organisations is present in the text. There is no evidence linking organisational support to project success, nor any mention of tailored support for the specific award type or career trajectory alignment.
sub_criteria.rationale: No specific description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the organisations is present in the text. There is no evidence linking organisational support to project success, nor any mention of tailored support for the specific award type or career trajectory alignment.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Inclusive and Supportive Research Culture
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that the organisation fosters inclusivity, research integrity, and a supportive environment for all researchers.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion
Evidence of inclusive recruitment, mentoring, or support initiatives
References to adherence to research integrity principles
Statements on organisational culture, training, or initiatives supporting underrepresented groups

sub_criteria.pros: The project title and team composition (including a survivor-led organisation) suggest an intent to be inclusive, but this is project-specific rather than organisational policy evidence.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The provided text contains no mentions of organisational policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion. There is no evidence of inclusive recruitment initiatives, adherence to research integrity principles, or statements on organisational culture supporting underrepresented groups.
sub_criteria.rationale: The provided text contains no mentions of organisational policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion. There is no evidence of inclusive recruitment initiatives, adherence to research integrity principles, or statements on organisational culture supporting underrepresented groups.

sub_criteria.name: Integration of Lived Experience in Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: How input from patients, carers, or people with relevant lived experience has shaped the research questions, design, and methods.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of consultation or co-design activities
Specific examples of changes made to study design based on lived experience
References to patient or community advisory groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates perfect integration of lived experience. It explicitly details co-design activities (focus groups, narrative interviews, co-design events) involving survivors and staff (secsa__001_b). Specific changes to the design are evidenced, such as the use of arts-based outputs (films) to feed into intervention development (seccfps__001_a). A formal advisory structure is present via the PPI partner 'Survivors Voices' who shape recruitment, intervention development, and data analysis (secppi__001_b).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan for changes is strong, the specific examples of *past* changes made to the study design based on prior lived experience input are described as part of the current proposal's methodology rather than retrospective changes to a previous version, though the 'Changes from Previous Stage' section confirms the PES was rewritten based on survivor advice (seccfps__001_e). This minor distinction prevents a perfect 5 on wp.1.b, though the evidence is very strong.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan for changes is strong, the specific examples of *past* changes made to the study design based on prior lived experience input are described as part of the current proposal's methodology rather than retrospective changes to a previous version, though the 'Changes from Previous Stage' section confirms the PES was rewritten based on survivor advice (seccfps__001_e). This minor distinction prevents a perfect 5 on wp.1.b, though the evidence is very strong.

sub_criteria.name: Diversity and Inclusivity of the Research Team
sub_criteria.definition: Extent to which the research team reflects diversity in terms of skills, experience, and demographic representation.

signals.signal_text: Explicit mentions of team composition across gender, ethnicity, age, or other relevant characteristics
Statements about inclusion in hiring or team-building
Evidence of equitable task allocation and collaboration

sub_criteria.pros: The team composition is diverse, including academic, clinical (NHS Trust), and community (Survivors Voices) representatives (secac__001). Task allocation is equitable and collaborative, with specific roles for the PPI partner in co-directing research and shaping methodological decisions (secppi__001_c). The inclusion of an 'Inclusion Lead' and interviewers reflecting participant identities further supports diversity (secsb__001_a).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit mention of the demographic characteristics (gender, ethnicity, age) of the research team members themselves, only the organizations they represent. Statements about inclusion in hiring are not present; the focus is on the project's inclusivity rather than the team's internal diversity metrics.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit mention of the demographic characteristics (gender, ethnicity, age) of the research team members themselves, only the organizations they represent. Statements about inclusion in hiring are not present; the focus is on the project's inclusivity rather than the team's internal diversity metrics.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Maximising Public and Patient Benefit
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity and strength of the plan to ensure research outcomes meaningfully benefit patients, carers, and the public.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of dissemination, knowledge translation, or implementation plans
Clear links between study objectives and anticipated benefits
Emphasis on actionable outcomes or improvements in health/care

sub_criteria.pros: Dissemination plans are robust, including sharing findings in accessible formats with community organizations and professional bodies (secsa__001_c). Creative outputs (films) are explicitly planned to explain findings and engage the public (secpesor__001_c). The link between objectives and benefits is clear: improving ED care to reduce distress and improve health outcomes for survivors.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan for dissemination is strong, the specific mechanisms for ensuring the *implementation* of the co-designed intervention in the NHS beyond the feasibility stage are described as a 'foundation' for future work rather than a guaranteed outcome of this specific award, slightly limiting the certainty of the benefit realization.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan for dissemination is strong, the specific mechanisms for ensuring the *implementation* of the co-designed intervention in the NHS beyond the feasibility stage are described as a 'foundation' for future work rather than a guaranteed outcome of this specific award, slightly limiting the certainty of the benefit realization.

sub_criteria.name: Representation of Seldom Heard or Underserved Groups
sub_criteria.definition: Degree to which participants and PPI contributors represent groups historically underrepresented in research, including those facing health inequalities.

signals.signal_text: Mention of specific demographic groups (age, race, ethnicity, disability, gender, sexual orientation, religion/belief, location, socioeconomic status)
Evidence of targeted recruitment strategies
Discussion of barriers to participation and mitigation strategies

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly identifies specific underserved groups including migrant women, cultural minorities, people with insecure housing, and those with mental health issues (secppi__001_c). Targeted recruitment strategies are detailed, including an Inclusion Lead, interviewers reflecting participant identities, and translation into 5 languages (secsb__001_c). Barriers to participation (e.g., language, cost, safety) are thoroughly discussed with specific mitigation strategies like travel reimbursement, childcare, and peer support (secsb__001_c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats found; the evidence for representation of seldom-heard groups is comprehensive and fully costed.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats found; the evidence for representation of seldom-heard groups is comprehensive and fully costed.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusivity Across the Research Lifecycle
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstration that inclusion is considered at all stages of the project: planning, recruitment, conduct, analysis, and dissemination.

signals.signal_text: References to inclusion in study design, participant selection, and analysis
Consideration of accessibility and equity in all project stages
Documented plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation

sub_criteria.pros: Inclusion is embedded across the entire lifecycle: from design (co-design events) to recruitment (inclusive strategies), conduct (safety measures, peer support), and dissemination (accessible formats) (secppi__001_b). Accessibility is considered through arts-based methods and specific support for diverse needs (secsa__001_b). The plan explicitly mentions monitoring and tackling barriers to participation (secppi__001_c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan for ongoing evaluation is present, the specific metrics or frequency for evaluating the *success* of the inclusion strategies themselves (beyond the research outcomes) are not explicitly detailed, preventing a perfect 5 on wp.5.c.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan for ongoing evaluation is present, the specific metrics or frequency for evaluating the *success* of the inclusion strategies themselves (beyond the research outcomes) are not explicitly detailed, preventing a perfect 5 on wp.5.c.

sub_criteria.name: Costed and Resourced Plan for Research Inclusion
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that research inclusion has been properly budgeted and resourced within the proposal.

signals.signal_text: Explicit budget line(s) for inclusion activities (PPI, outreach, recruitment support)
Mention of staff time, materials, or training dedicated to inclusion
Alignment between inclusion plan and allocated resources

sub_criteria.pros: The budget explicitly includes line items for inclusion activities: PPI partner time (increased to 13%), inclusion lead, interpreters, translation, travel for carers, and participant care costs (secsb__001_c). Staff time is dedicated to inclusion (Inclusion Lead, additional interviewers) (secsb__001_a). The alignment between the inclusion plan and resources is perfect, with specific costs justified for every inclusion strategy mentioned (seccfps__001_e).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No drawbacks; the costed plan is comprehensive and directly supports the inclusion strategies outlined.
sub_criteria.rationale: No drawbacks; the costed plan is comprehensive and directly supports the inclusion strategies outlined.

sub_criteria.name: Use of Formatting, Headings, and Subheadings
sub_criteria.definition: Effective use of text formatting to make the application clear, navigable, and easy to read.

signals.signal_text: Presence and consistency of headings and subheadings
Use of bold or emphasis for key terms
Structured hierarchy of sections (logical nesting of headings)
Patterns detected via text parsing for formatting markers

sub_criteria.pros: The application form analysis confirms the presence of 7 distinct sections with clear hierarchy (numbered_heading_like_lines=7) and 26 list markers, indicating strong structural organization. Extracted emphasis markers (43) suggest effective use of formatting to highlight key terms, though parser limitations mean some bolding may be lost.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While emphasis markers are detected, the parser note indicates that bold/emphasis may be lost during extraction, preventing a perfect score for af.1.b as the visual weight of specific terms cannot be fully verified.
sub_criteria.rationale: While emphasis markers are detected, the parser note indicates that bold/emphasis may be lost during extraction, preventing a perfect score for af.1.b as the visual weight of specific terms cannot be fully verified.

sub_criteria.name: Limited Duplication and Repetition
sub_criteria.definition: Avoidance of unnecessary repetition within or across sections.

signals.signal_text: Semantic similarity measures between sentences/paragraphs
Detection of repeated phrases or ideas
Novelty score for each section (unique content vs. overlap)

sub_criteria.pros: Duplication metrics are excellent, with a duplicate sentence rate of 0.008 and repeated line rate of 0.008, indicating minimal unnecessary repetition. Lexical overlap between sections is low (0.189-0.235), suggesting distinct content across the Scientific Abstract, Changes from Previous Stage, and Budget sections.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While overlap is low, the presence of any lexical overlap (up to 23.5%) between the Summary Budget and Application Context suggests some inevitable repetition of financial figures or context, preventing a perfect score for novelty.
sub_criteria.rationale: While overlap is low, the presence of any lexical overlap (up to 23.5%) between the Summary Budget and Application Context suggests some inevitable repetition of financial figures or context, preventing a perfect score for novelty.

sub_criteria.name: Logical Flow and Coherence Across Sections
sub_criteria.definition: The application progresses logically, with a clear thread linking the research question, objectives, and work packages.

signals.signal_text: Semantic coherence between sections (using embeddings or similarity metrics)
Explicit linking of objectives to methods and work packages
Presence of transitional phrases or linking statements
Detectable narrative thread anchored on research aims

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates strong logical flow with 21 transition phrases and 42 terms linking objectives, methods, and budget. The section order follows a logical progression from applicant context to research plan, PPI, and budget, supporting a coherent narrative thread.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the structural indicators for flow are strong, the analysis relies on keyword counts and section ordering rather than deep semantic embedding analysis of the narrative thread itself, leaving a minor gap in verifying the depth of the narrative connection.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the structural indicators for flow are strong, the analysis relies on keyword counts and section ordering rather than deep semantic embedding analysis of the narrative thread itself, leaving a minor gap in verifying the depth of the narrative connection.
